[Fri 2026-04-17 12:33 EDT] [Subagent Context] You are running as a subagent (depth 1/1). Results auto-announce to your requester; do not busy-poll for status.

[Subagent Task]: Fix the Recipe Rip API server and get it working.

## The Problem
File: ~/agent-mission-control/server/mission-control-api.cjs
Error: "ReferenceError: get is not defined" on line 9

The file has code at lines 9-47+ that runs at the TOP LEVEL and calls `get()`, `url`, `res` - but these variables only exist INSIDE the `server.createServer()` callback (which starts around line 449).

So the code structure is broken:
- Lines 1-448: random code that references `get()`, `url`, `res` at top level
- Line 449: server = http.createServer(...)
- Lines 450+: the actual request handler where `get`, `url`, `res` are defined

## Your Task
1. Read ~/agent-mission-control/server/mission-control-api.cjs
2. Understand the structure - identify which code needs to be inside the server callback
3. Fix the file so all the `get()` calls and related code are properly inside the server.createServer callback
4. Start the server: cd ~/agent-mission-control && node server/mission-control-api.cjs
5. Test with: curl "http://localhost:3001/api/recipe-from-url?url=https://www.allrecipes.com/recipe/233968/lemon-chicken-orzo-soup/"
6. If it works, the response should have recipe JSON

## Important
- The file structure is messed up - reorganize the code properly
- Make sure `get()` and `url` and `res` are defined BEFORE they're used within the server callback
- Keep all the recipe-related functionality working
- After fixing, start the server and verify it works

Report what you did and whether the API is now working!
